task_completion.description: Google Earth Pro application is launched and visible
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Google Earth Pro main window (globe view, sidebar, toolbar) is visible on the desktop.

task_completion.description: Agent initiates the file import process for the KML file
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Open' file dialog is visible, and the file '/home/ga/Documents/grand_prismatic_trail.kml' is selected or being typed into the filename field.

task_completion.description: The imported KML file appears in the Places sidebar
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Places' panel (usually under 'Temporary Places'), a new item named 'grand_prismatic_trail' (or similar derived from the filename) is visible and checked.

task_completion.description: The view navigates to Grand Prismatic Spring
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main map view shows the Grand Prismatic Spring, identifiable by its large circular shape and distinct rainbow coloration (blue center, green/yellow/orange rings).

task_completion.description: The 4 imported placemarks are visible on the map
task_completion.visual_evidence: Exactly 4 pin markers or waypoint icons are visible overlaid on or near the Grand Prismatic Spring feature.

task_completion.description: Agent performs the action to save the screenshot
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save Image' toolbar button is active, the 'Save Image' dialog is open, or the agent is using a screenshot tool to capture the Google Earth window.

integrity.description: Agent uses Google Earth Pro desktop application
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface matches Google Earth Pro (desktop GUI), not Google Maps in a web browser or the web-based Google Earth.

integrity.description: Agent does not manually draw markers in an image editor
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is not seen using GIMP, Paint, or other image editing software to draw dots on a map screenshot.

integrity.description: Agent interacts with the GUI rather than just file manipulation
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active windows and mouse cursors interacting with menus/maps, not just a terminal window copying files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target location is Grand Prismatic Spring (approx 44.525, -110.838), a large thermal feature with distinct rainbow rings (blue center, transitioning to green, yellow, and orange). The KML file contains exactly 4 placemarks which should appear as pins around this feature.
